series_description: Documents generated by or that come into the possession of a deputy inspector general during the course of an investigation that are not relied on in coming to any conclusion in the investigative report, or are determined to be outside the scope of the investigation are considered as having no value and will be discarded at the proper time and in accordance with the record retention schedule.
retention_statement: Paper: Until no longer of administrative value ; Electronic: Until no longer of administrative value

series_description: Consist of complaint form and supporting documentation, status notification letters, referral letters and any and all documents used to determine whether there is reasonable cause to believe the alleged wrongful act or omission has been committed or is being committed by a state officer or employee.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 4 years after complaint file is closed. ; Electronic: Retain 4 years after complaint file is closed.

series_description: consist of complaint form and supporting documents, notification letters, referral letters, subpoenas, and any and all documents necessary to establish the scope and completeness of the investigation. Investigation files may also contain confidential information.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 4 years after case is closed ; Electronic: Retain 4 years after case is closed

series_description: consist of documents for potential new hires of the Inspector General’s Office that are part of the pre-hire screening process. Documents can include but are not limited to: drug test documents and results, criminal background check and court documents, reference check and/or field investigation results, limited tax waiver compliance review.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 1 year from employment start date or hiring decision, whichever is later
